explicit_text: I enlisted in the United States Air Force Reserve on December 1, 1964, because I liked the Air Force best among the military branches and wanted to work on aircraft. I went through basic training at Lackland Air Force Base and tech school at Shepherd Air Force Base. Later, in 1968, my unit, the 930th Tactical Airlift Group at Bakalar Air Force Base, was activated by the Secretary of Defense during the Vietnam War, and we transitioned to a special operations mission flying gunships.

explicit_text: I went to basic training at Lackland Air Force Base in San Antonio, Texas, starting in January 1965. The six weeks there were tough both mentally and physically, but I managed by keeping quiet and doing what was required. It was my first real experience in the Air Force Reserve after enlisting in December 1964.

explicit_text: During my time in the Air Force Reserve, the Vietnam War was ongoing. I initially thought I might avoid deployment, but in 1968, the Secretary of Defense activated many reservists, including my unit, the 930th Tactical Airlift Group. We were then trained and equipped to support the war effort with gunship aircraft before eventually deploying.

explicit_text: After basic training, I attended tech school at Shepherd Air Force Base in Wichita Falls, Texas, around early 1965. The 13- to 14-week program taught me how to be a mechanic on reciprocating engine aircraft, which fit my mechanical skills well. This training prepared me for my role in the Air Force Reserve.

explicit_text: Before and after my initial Air Force Reserve training, I was attending Purdue Extension in Indianapolis. explicit_text: On April 11, 1968, the Secretary of Defense announced the activation of about 24,500 reservists and National Guard members, including my unit, the 930th Tactical Airlift Group at Bakalar Air Force Base. This call-up changed everything for us, leading to active duty and a new mission during the Vietnam War. It was a pivotal moment in my military service.
implicit_text: On April 11, 1968, the Secretary of Defense announced the activation of about 24,500 reservists and National Guard members, including my unit at the Indiana air base. This call-up changed everything for us, leading to active duty and a new mission during the Vietnam War. It was a pivotal moment in my military service.

explicit_text: I was assigned to the 930th Tactical Airlift Group at Bakalar Air Force Base in Indiana when the Secretary of Defense activated our unit in May 1968. We reported for active duty there on May 13, 1968, but didn’t know our mission yet. Shortly after, we packed up and moved to Lockbourne Air Force Base in Ohio for our new special operations role.
implicit_text: I was assigned to the unit at the Indiana air base when the Secretary of Defense activated our unit in May 1968. We reported for active duty there on May 13, 1968, but didn’t know our mission yet. Shortly after, we packed up and moved to the Ohio air base near Columbus for our new special operations role.

explicit_text: I was part of the 930th Tactical Airlift Group based at Bakalar Air Force Base when we were activated in May 1968. This group was unique as the only Air Force Reserve unit flying C-119 aircraft that was called up. We transitioned from cargo missions to special operations gunship roles during our time at Lockbourne Air Force Base.
implicit_text: I was part of the tactical airlift group based at the Indiana air base when we were activated in May 1968. This group was unique as the only Air Force Reserve unit flying C-119 aircraft that was called up. We transitioned from cargo missions to special operations gunship roles during our time at the Ohio air base.

explicit_text: Our unit flew the C-119 Flying Boxcar, which was originally a cargo aircraft. After activation in 1968, we converted these planes into gunships to replace the older AC-47 Spooky gunships used in Vietnam. The transition and training on the C-119 gunships took place during the summer of 1968 at Lockbourne Air Force Base.

explicit_text: After activation, my unit moved from Bakalar Air Force Base to Lockbourne Air Force Base near Columbus, Ohio, around mid-June 1968. There, we learned about our new mission converting C-119 cargo planes into gunships and became the 71st Special Operations Squadron. We trained extensively throughout the summer of 1968 before deploying.

explicit_text: Lockbourne Air Force Base, where my unit was transferred in 1968, is located on the south side of Columbus, Ohio. This city became the base for our transition to special operations and gunship training during the Vietnam War. Columbus was where we prepared for our new mission with the 71st Special Operations Squadron.

explicit_text: The base we moved to in Ohio was Lockbourne Air Force Base, which is now known as Rickenbacker Air National Guard Base. At the time of our transfer in 1968, it was still Lockbourne, but I know it has since been renamed. This base was central to our training and preparation for the gunship mission.

At the time of our transfer in 1968, it was still Lockbourne, but I know it has since been renamed. This base was central to our training and preparation for the gunship mission.

explicit_text: When we were first activated in May 1968, our unit was called the 71st Tactical Airlift Squadron. Shortly after arriving at Lockbourne Air Force Base, the name changed as our mission evolved. This squadron was the starting point before we became the 71st Air Commando Squadron and then the 71st Special Operations Squadron.
implicit_text: When we were first activated in May 1968, our unit was called the 71st Tactical Airlift Squadron. Shortly after arriving at the Ohio air base, the name changed as our mission evolved. This squadron was the starting point before we became the Air Commando Squadron and then the Special Operations Squadron.

explicit_text: After arriving at Lockbourne Air Force Base in 1968, our unit was redesignated the 71st Air Commando Squadron for about three weeks. Then, on July 8, 1968, the Air Commando Command was renamed Special Operations, and our squadron became the 71st Special Operations Squadron. This brief period marked the transition to our new special operations role.
implicit_text: After arriving at the Ohio air base in 1968, our unit was redesignated the Air Commando Squadron for about three weeks. This brief period marked the transition to our new special operations role.

explicit_text: The Air Commando Command was the organization overseeing our unit when we first became the 71st Air Commando Squadron in 1968. However, on July 8, 1968, the entire command was renamed Special Operations, reflecting a broader mission. This change coincided with our unit becoming the 71st Special Operations Squadron.

explicit_text: From July 8, 1968, until our return home in 1969, our unit was known as the 71st Special Operations Squadron. We flew converted C-119 gunships and trained extensively at Lockbourne Air Force Base for our special operations missions during the Vietnam War. This squadron represented the culmination of our transition from a tactical airlift unit to a specialized combat role.
implicit_text: From July 8, 1968, until our return home in 1969, our unit was known as the Special Operations Squadron. This squadron represented the culmination of our transition from a tactical airlift unit to a specialized combat role.

explicit_text: Before we transitioned to the C-119 gunships, the AC-47 Spooky was the gunship aircraft used in Vietnam. The Air Force wanted a better platform, so our unit was tasked with converting the C-119 Flying Boxcar to replace the AC-47. This was a significant upgrade and part of our special operations mission starting in 1968.

explicit_text: I chose to enlist in the United States Air Force Reserve in late 1964 because I admired the Air Force among the military branches and wanted to work on aircraft. Throughout my service, including basic training at Lackland and tech school at Shepherd, I was proud to be part of the Air Force. During the Vietnam War, my unit was activated and transitioned to special operations flying gunship aircraft, representing the Air Force's evolving combat roles.
